one_line_thesis: AJG is a global insurance broker with sticky commission revenue, a long acquisition track record, and durable local-agency relationships.

deep_dive.executive_summary: AJG is a global insurance broker with sticky commission revenue, a long acquisition track record, and durable local-agency relationships. FY2025 free cash flow per share is about $6.72 after estimated maintenance capex. At ~$254 (July 2026), total synthesis implies 2.29% per year over 7 years on filing-based owner cash. Moat is stable (switching costs, local relationships) but not wide. Partial dhando because net debt (~$47 per share) and AssuredPartners integration risk cap the bear case. Proof-first component sum ~$102/sh vs price signals rich valuation. Marvin proposes watch on math; [HU...

pricing_analysis.conclusion: Hurdle prices are derived from the decision-grade universal contract; they do not constitute a capital decision.

decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

optionality.rule: Every option requires a beneficiary, probability, timing, remaining capital, failure case, and non-overlap control; absence of option value must be explicit rather than unvalued.

committee.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

method_fit.label: High-return compounder
applicability_tests: Owner earnings can be separated from stock compensation, working capital, and maintenance capital.
Incremental returns on retained capital are observable and durable.
The competitive advantage explains pricing, retention, share, or cost outcomes.
Terminal value is supported by a reinvestment runway rather than an unexplained multiple.
failure_modes: growth projected without its capital cost
terminal value unsupported by a durable mechanism

validation_cohort.purpose: Test depletion, commodity exposure, finite reserves, and royalty-unit reconciliation.
Test tangible capital, credit losses, funding, regulation, and excess-capital realization.
Test owner earnings, capital-light reinvestment, cyclicality, and buyback value creation.
Test normalized margins, industry supply response, replacement cost, and reinvestment timing.
Test product concentration, pipeline probabilities, patent cliffs, and catalyst timing.

method_fit.rule: Use one primary method and no more than two corroborating methods; irrelevant methods and personas remain silent.

outcomes.weighting_rule: Do not change a persona's relevance within a power zone until at least 20 attributable, dividend-aware outcomes exist there; never reweight automatically.

attribution.explanation: The current component schedule is the first decision-grade baseline; attribution begins with the next dated valuation snapshot.

component_valuation.decision_rule: Use the complete low/base/high component schedule. Embedded components are estimated but not added twice.

components.label: Brokerage and risk-management owner-cash engine
components.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K before decision use.

components.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K before decision use.

components.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K before decision use.

components.label: Integration, earnout, and leverage stress reserve
components.cross_check: Reconcile to FY2025 10-K before decision use.

economic_value.accounting_reference: FY2025 10-K: stockholders' equity $23.3B; economic value in normalized owner cash ($6.723/sh), not GAAP book alone.

economic_claim.description: One diluted share of AJG, including brokerage/risk-management owner cash, AssuredPartners integration upside, net financial claims, and integration reserve.
economic_claim.enterprise_to_equity_reconciliation: Consolidated brokerage engine valued through owner-cash discount on FY2025 FCF per share; AssuredPartners option, net liquidity, and integration reserve are separate overlap keys.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Normalized free cash flow from brokerage and risk-management segments
valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from AJG/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260217_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_008662.htm: FY2025 revenue $13942.0M, OCF $1930.0M, FCF $1750.0M, cash $1155.0M, total debt $13513.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Fiduciary pass-through excluded; maintenance capex estimated from depreciation.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Incremental cross-sell and EBITDAC lift from AssuredPartners acquisition
valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from AJG/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260217_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_008662.htm: FY2025 revenue $13942.0M, OCF $1930.0M, FCF $1750.0M, cash $1155.0M, total debt $13513.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Not in Lawrence base FCF path; $3.56B annualized revenue at close.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

risk_and_timing.timing_basis: Synergy realization over 2–4 years per FY2025 10-K acquisition disclosures.
risk_and_timing.probability_basis: Base ~35% that integration delivers planned cross-sell; low case zero.
risk_and_timing.remaining_capital_basis: Integration capex and earnouts funded from operating cash and debt.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: Net corporate liquidity after short-term borrowings, long-term debt, and operating minimum
valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from AJG/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260217_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_008662.htm: FY2025 revenue $13942.0M, OCF $1930.0M, FCF $1750.0M, cash $1155.0M, total debt $13513.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Fiduciary cash excluded; net debt ~$12.4B at FY2025.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

valuation_proof.economic_claim: AssuredPartners integration risk and interest-expense step-up stress
valuation_proof.evidence: Primary bridge from AJG/investor-documents/sec-edgar/10-K_20260217_rpt20251231_acc0001628280_26_008662.htm: FY2025 revenue $13942.0M, OCF $1930.0M, FCF $1750.0M, cash $1155.0M, total debt $13513.0M; contract backfill 2026-07-21.
valuation_proof.adjustment: Interest expense $639M FY2025 (+68% YoY) caps partial dhando.
valuation_proof.falsifier: Primary evidence shows claim, cash conversion, or capital structure is materially worse than low case.

total_return_panel.return_contract: split-adjusted close excluding dividends; event amounts normalized for later splits; cash reinvested at ex-date close; pre-tax nominal

valuation_decision.next_action: Freeze the evidence packet and select three method-independent raters.

timeline.label: Closed AssuredPartners acquisition (~$13.8B consideration); FY2025 M&A cash outflow $15.77B; annualized revenue $3.56B; goodwill $22.6B
FY2025 results: revenue $13.94B (+21%); net income $1.49B; OCF $1.93B; interest expense $639M (+68% YoY); diluted EPS $5.74
FY2025 dividends $667M; corporate cash fell to $1.16B from $14.76B prior year after acquisition funding
FY2024: revenue $11.56B; net income $1.46B; OCF $2.58B; diluted EPS $6.50; pre-AssuredPartners baseline
Announced AssuredPartners transaction; S-3ASR shelf for acquisition financing
Continued tuck-in M&A strategy; brokerage segment revenue $5.87B FY2022 with global correspondent network expansion
J. Patrick Gallagher Jr. remains chairman and CEO; long-tenured leadership through serial acquisition cycle
Arthur J. Gallagher & Co. founded as insurance agency; evolved into global brokerage and risk-management franchise listed on NYSE

industry.structure: Global insurance brokerage is concentrated among Marsh (MRSH), Aon (AON), WTW, and AJG; local and specialty brokers fill long tail. Commission revenue tied to insured exposure and policy rates; minimal balance-sheet insurance risk.
industry.share_shift: AJG gained scale via AssuredPartners (2025) and steady tuck-ins; Marsh and Aon remain larger globally but AJG closes gap in US middle market and specialty lines.
industry.trend: Commercial insurance pricing cycle (hard/soft market) drives commission growth; interest expense rises with acquisition debt; consolidation continues as brokers roll up local agencies.
